Review some points about MS data

Dynamic range of signals

Dynamic range describes the span between the smallest and largest signal a system can detect or measure reliably.
It is usually expressed in orders of magnitude (powers of 10).

For example: - A dynamic range of \(10^{6}\) means the largest signal is 1,000,000× bigger than the smallest detectable one. - In biology, dynamic range is critical because molecules can exist at vastly different concentrations in the same sample.

  • Mount Damavand: \(\sim 5.6 \times 10^{3}\) m
  • Fruit fly: \(\sim 3 \times 10^{-3}\) m
  • Ratio: \(\sim 10^{6}\) (6 orders of magnitude)

Detection technologies

System / Technology Dynamic range (orders) Molecular coverage Physical scale (meters / concentration) Damavand analogy
Human eye (adapted) \(\sim 10^{9}\)–\(10^{12}\) Macroscopic scene Brightness: \(\sim 10^{-6}\) to \(10^{4}\) cd/m² From sunlight to starlight (time-adapted)
Mass spectrometry \(\sim 10^{4}\)–\(10^{6}\) \(\sim 10^{3}\)–\(10^{5}\) proteins \(\sim 10^{-12}\) to \(10^{-6}\) M (pM–µM) Damavand (\(5.6 \times 10^{3}\) m) → fruit fly (\(3 \times 10^{-3}\) m)
General chemical assays (UV, fluorescence, NMR) \(\sim 10^{3}\)–\(10^{5}\) 1–10 analytes \(\sim 10^{-6}\) to \(10^{-3}\) M (µM–mM) Damavand → building scale
Biological proteome (human plasma) \(\sim 10^{10}\)–\(10^{12}\) \(\sim 10^{4}\)–\(10^{5}\) proteins \(\sim 10^{-3}\) to \(10^{-15}\) M (mM–fM) Damavand → sub-viral / molecular scale

Signal representation in MS

  • The continuous workflow of separating-separating-separating!

  • Three dimensions: Retention time of analyser elution - Mass to charge (M/Z) - Intensity

Spectrum is the unit of mass spectrometry data

TIC (total ion chromatogram) plot: The intensities of all ions in each mass spectrometer scan (within a given time period) are summed and plotted as a function of time. Base peak chromatogram plot: The intensity of the base peak (the most intense ion in each mass spectrum) is plotted as a function of time (Note that the base peak should not be confused with the baseline, which represents the constant background signal of the instrument). XIC or EIC plot: The intensity of a specific analyte at a chosen m/z value (either total or base peak intensity) is isolated and plotted as a function of time.

Chromatogram plots

Group Activity

Check this file “https://github.com/jafarilab/MSProt2026/blob/main/GroupActivity_20Apr.qmd”.

Getting data

Get to know the ProteomeXchange Consortium: Established to create globally coordinated, standardized pipelines for data submission and dissemination across major proteomics repositories, the Consortium promotes open data policies in proteomics.

proteomexchange.org

#Direct accessing data
library(rpx)
library(tidyr)
px <- PXDataset("PXD022816")
pxget(px, grep("mzID", pxfiles(px))[1:3]) # It might take time for the local access  
pxget(px, grep("mzML", pxfiles(px))[1:3]) # It might take time for the local access 

(px <- PXDataset("PXD000001"))
(pxfiles(px)) # file names of the object

fn <- "TMT_Erwinia_1uLSike_Top10HCD_isol2_45stepped_60min_01-20141210.mzML"
mzf <- pxget(px, fn)

pxtax(px) # taxonomic name
pxref(px) # bibliography ref



pxget(px, "erwinia_carotovora.fasta")
pxget(px, "README.txt") # a vector defining the files to be found, try to find it on your device.
# Some files are accessible remotely and some of them from local cashing mechanism.
pxfiles(px) %>% str() # To check 

(f <- pxget(px, "TMT_Erwinia_1uLSike_Top10HCD_isol2_45stepped_60min_01-20141210.mzML"))
basename(f)

MS example data: msdata

# Various Mass Spectrometry raw data example files for proteomics and metabolomics
library(msdata) 
proteomics()
## [1] "MRM-standmix-5.mzML.gz"                                                
## [2] "MS3TMT10_01022016_32917-33481.mzML.gz"                                 
## [3] "MS3TMT11.mzML"                                                         
## [4] "TMT_Erwinia_1uLSike_Top10HCD_isol2_45stepped_60min_01-20141210.mzML.gz"
## [5] "TMT_Erwinia_1uLSike_Top10HCD_isol2_45stepped_60min_01.mzML.gz"
ident()
## [1] "TMT_Erwinia_1uLSike_Top10HCD_isol2_45stepped_60min_01-20141210.mzid"
quant()
## [1] "cptac_a_b_peptides.txt"

Quiz 1 (GA_4)

Visit gosocrative.com and enter room name FIN2026

Raw MS data

  • We need to convert binary-vendor-specific formats to open formats such as mzML, mzXML.

  • mzML is the most recent format.

  • proteowizard msconvert: https://proteowizard.sourceforge.io/ or search in Google msconvert GUI

  • ThermoRawFileParser: https://github.com/compomics/ThermoRawFileParser

  • To check all MS-related data format: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mass_spectrometry_data_format
